name: Kraken
what_it_does: Cryptocurrency exchange REST and WebSocket API providing real-time market data, order management, account balances, and trade execution with HMAC-signed authenticated endpoints.
use_cases: Execute automated trading strategies by placing, modifying, and cancelling limit/market orders via the private REST API
Stream real-time order book and trade ticker data over WebSocket for low-latency market analysis without polling
Monitor portfolio balances and open positions across all assets to trigger rebalancing logic when allocation drifts beyond thresholds
Pull historical OHLCV (candlestick) data for multiple trading pairs to feed backtesting and ML model training pipelines
Automate staking and unstaking of proof-of-stake assets based on yield rate thresholds and liquidity requirements
not_for: Fiat-only payment processing — Kraken is a crypto exchange, not a payment gateway for traditional currency transactions
High-frequency trading requiring microsecond latency — Kraken's API is not a co-location trading API; use dedicated HFT infrastructure
Retail users in jurisdictions where Kraken is not licensed — geographic restrictions on API access apply
best_when: An automated trading agent needs to execute orders and stream market data from a reputable, regulated exchange with a well-documented REST and WebSocket API.
avoid_when: Your use case requires DeFi or on-chain transactions — Kraken is a centralized exchange and does not expose smart contract or blockchain interaction APIs.

auth.notes: Private API endpoints require an API key (public key) plus an HMAC-SHA512 signature generated from the request payload + nonce using the API secret (private key). Scopes (permissions) are set at key creation time: Query, Trade, Withdraw, etc. Withdrawal permission must be explicitly enabled — it is off by default. API keys can be restricted by IP address for additional security.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Public market data API (order book, tickers, OHLCV) is free with no authentication required. Private APIs (trading, balances) require a funded account.
pricing.paid_tiers: No direct API subscription fee. Trading fees apply per trade: 0.16% maker / 0.26% taker for spot, lower for high-volume accounts. Pro and institutional fee tiers available.
pricing.notes: API access is free; costs are purely trading fees on executed orders. Kraken Pro (futures) has separate fee schedules. Staking rewards are earned automatically with no API fee.

performance.rate_limits: REST API: 15-20 calls per minute for private endpoints (counter-based system that replenishes over time). WebSocket: no hard rate limit on subscriptions but connection limits apply. Exceeding REST rate limits returns error code EAPI:Rate limit exceeded.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns a JSON array of errors under the 'error' key (e.g., ['EAPI:Invalid key', 'EOrder:Insufficient funds']). Error codes are well-documented and machine-parseable. WebSocket error messages are also structured. Error strings are prefixed with category codes (EAPI, EOrder, EFunding) enabling programmatic handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order placement is not idempotent — the same request submitted twice creates two separate orders. Agents must track order IDs and check for existing open orders before placing new ones to prevent duplicate trades.
known_agent_gotchas: HMAC signature generation requires precise nonce management — nonces must be strictly increasing integers; using timestamps in microseconds is recommended but clock skew causes signature failures
Kraken's rate limit system uses a counter (not a sliding window) that increments differently per endpoint — agents using simple request counting will hit limits unexpectedly
WebSocket authenticated feeds require a separate WebSocket auth token obtained via REST API (/0/private/GetWebSocketsToken) that expires after 15 minutes
Order pair names differ between REST API ('XXBTZUSD') and WebSocket ('XBT/USD') — using the wrong format causes silent failures or 'Unknown asset pair' errors
Withdrawal endpoint is disabled on new API keys by default and requires explicit activation in the dashboard — agents testing withdrawals will receive permission errors even with valid keys

security_score_components.security_notes: HMAC-SHA512 signing prevents replay attacks and provides strong request integrity. Granular permission scopes (Query, Trade, Withdraw) allow least-privilege key creation. IP allowlisting is available and strongly recommended for trading bots. The API secret is shown only once at key creation — loss requires key rotation.
